author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
Pelagius Teaches that Satan May Be Resisted Without the Help of the Grace of God.
title: Pelagius Teaches that Satan May Be Resisted Without the Help of the Grace of God.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Chapter 28 [XXVII.]—Pelagius Teaches that Satan May Be Resisted Without the Help of the Grace of God.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: In the book which he addressed to a certain holy virgin, there is a passage which I have already mentioned, wherein he plainly indicates what he holds on this subject; for he speaks of our “deserving the grace of God, and by the help of the Holy Ghost more easily resisting the evil spirit.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Now why did he insert the phrase “more easily”?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Was not the sense already complete: “And by the help of the Holy Ghost resisting the evil spirit”?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: But who can fail to perceive what an injury he has done by this insertion?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: He wants it, of course, to be supposed, that so great are the powers of our nature, which he is in such a hurry to exalt, that even without the assistance of the Holy Ghost the evil spirit can be resisted—less easily it may be, but still in a certain measure.
